The //fabrik:job and //fabrik:cron directives build and own *jobs.Manager
(bound by path). An app //fabrik:provider returning the same type would be
silently shadowed by Instance's resolution order, so it is rejected.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli => CLIDIR

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs => JOBSDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- shared/shared.go --
package shared

import (
	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli"
)

//fabrik:provider
func NewJobStore() (jobs.Store, error) { return jobs.NewMemoryStore(), nil }

//fabrik:provider
func NewManager(s jobs.Store) *jobs.Manager {
	m, _ := jobs.New(s, jobs.Config{})
	return m
}

//fabrik:cli:command
func Boot(ctx cli.Context, mgr *jobs.Manager) error { return nil }
-- mail/mail.go --
package mail

import "context"

type Welcome struct{ UserID int64 }

//fabrik:job
func SendWelcome(ctx context.Context, m Welcome) error { return nil }
-- want/diags --
error: mail/mail.go:7:1: an app provider returns *jobs.Manager, but //fabrik:job and //fabrik:cron already build and own the manager
  help: remove that provider; the store and jobs.Config providers still apply
